channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Release Date Cued In for July
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo has announced the release date for upcoming rhythm game Rhythm Heaven Groove, with the latest entry in the long-running musical minigame series set to arrive on Switch in just a couple of months.

As announced via the Nintendo Today app (and subsequently on social media), Rhythm Heaven Groove will be launching on July 2nd. It's a Switch exclusive, and it doesn't look like the game will have a native Switch 2 version, although it'll work just fine on the newer console.

If you're not familiar with Rhythm Heaven Groove, it's the latest game in the Rhythm Heaven series, which, as the name suggests, primarily revolves around rhythm-based minigames. As the debut trailer for the game says, there's no "big, epic story" here, just "lots of funny little rhythm games".

Since there's no story in this series, you won't find the continuation of some decades-long narrative in Rhythm Heaven Groove. Rather, what you'll find is some more fun ways to express your sense of rhythm, whether that be by hitting cans in a factory, chopping up vegetables, or jumping through hoops.

If you love minigame collections like the WarioWare series (whose longtime producer Goro Abe recently quit Nintendo after more than two decades, incidentally), then Rhythm Heaven Groove will almost certainly be the game for you, just as long as you've got a decent sense of rhythm.

Even if you haven't, there will be a visual representation of what you're supposed to be doing on screen, although given the name of the game and the insanely catchy nature of the music in previous entries, you'll want the sound cranked up for this one.

Rhythm Heaven Groove will launch exclusively for Nintendo Switch (although it'll be fully playable on Switch 2 as well) on July 2nd. Keep that date in mind if you're looking for some uncomplicated fun on your Switch.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
